commissioners.name: Rebecca Martinez
commissioners.title: City Commissioner
commissioners.bio: District 1 Commissioner. Passionate about affordable housing and neighborhood revitalization. Rebecca has lived in the North End for 15 years and advocates for equitable development.
commissioners.meetingSchedule: City Commission: 1st & 3rd Monday, 6:00 PM at City Hall
commissioners.officeHours: Tuesdays 2-4 PM, Thursdays 10 AM-12 PM (City Hall, Room 202)
commissioners.address: City Hall, 220 W Central Ave, El Dorado, KS 67042

votingRecord.reason: Critical for walkability and mixed-income housing

commissioners.districtDescription: North and Northeast El Dorado - includes downtown, university district, and emerging neighborhoods north of Central Ave

commissioners.name: James Park
commissioners.title: City Commissioner
commissioners.bio: District 2 Commissioner focused on economic development and industrial recruitment. James represents the business community and has 20 years of manufacturing experience.
commissioners.meetingSchedule: City Commission: 1st & 3rd Monday, 6:00 PM at City Hall
commissioners.officeHours: Wednesdays 1-3 PM, Saturdays 9-11 AM (Park's Hardware Store, Main St)
commissioners.address: City Hall, 220 W Central Ave, El Dorado, KS 67042

votingRecord.reason: Strategic industrial zoning and job creation

commissioners.districtDescription: South and Southeast El Dorado - includes industrial corridor, commercial areas, and residential south of Central Ave

commissioners.name: Susan Chen
commissioners.title: City Commissioner
commissioners.bio: District 3 Commissioner. Advocates for parks, trails, and environmental sustainability. Susan is a retired environmental scientist and works with regional conservation groups.
commissioners.meetingSchedule: City Commission: 1st & 3rd Monday, 6:00 PM at City Hall
commissioners.officeHours: Thursdays 3-5 PM, Sundays 2-4 PM (El Dorado Lake Pavilion, seasonal)
commissioners.address: City Hall, 220 W Central Ave, El Dorado, KS 67042

votingRecord.reason: Regional connectivity and healthy community outcomes
Recreation and wildlife habitat with proper environmental stewardship
Cultural enrichment and green building standards
Climate action and reduced carbon footprint
Mixed-use development with green space integration

commissioners.districtDescription: West and Southwest El Dorado - includes residential neighborhoods, El Dorado Lake area, and park/recreation facilities

voteDescriptions.abstain: Abstained - Did not vote / Conflict of interest
